---
title: "Why test?"
subtitle: "Testing training — Module 01"
lang: en
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    code-block-height: 400px
---

## 🇫🇷

[🇫🇷 Version française](../../slides/01_intro.qmd)

---

## A pipeline can succeed… and produce wrong output

Real case — [anvi'o blog 2022](https://anvio.org/blog/bioinformatics-bugs/)

. . .

- Two tools annotate the same genes with the same KOfam database
- Expected: limited differences, explainable by method
- Observed: up to **65 % false positives**

. . .

The pipeline runs. The files are generated. **No visible error.**

---

## The minimal test: protecting the business rule

**Bug 1 — filtering with the wrong bit score threshold**

Rule: a hit passes the filter only if its score ≥ the model's threshold.

```
KO_A  threshold = 100,  score = 120  → kept    ✓
KO_B  threshold = 500,  score =  80  → rejected ✗
```

. . .

```python
# Bad test
assert os.path.exists("output.filtered")

# Good test — protects the business rule
assert all(hit.score >= threshold[hit.ko] for hit in kept_hits)
```

---

## What a test provides

- **Early detection** — bug found before merge, not in production
- **Living documentation** — the test says what the code is supposed to do
- **Fear-free refactoring** — if tests pass, the tested behaviours are preserved
- **CI possible** — real automation

---

## The team rule

::: {.callout-important}
**No merge without a test.**

Every bug found → a test that would have caught it.

Every module → a `.nf.test` file.
:::

---

## The tools {.smaller}

| Language | Framework |
|---------|-----------|
| Nextflow | [nf-test](https://www.nf-test.com/) |
| Bash | [bats](https://bats-core.readthedocs.io/) |
| Python | [pytest](https://docs.pytest.org/) |
| R | [testthat](https://testthat.r-lib.org/) |

---

## What tests do not guarantee

- That the biology is correct
- That the parameters are optimal
- That the third-party tool works without bugs
- That there are no other bugs

. . .

They automatically verify the behaviours that have been explicitly defined.

---

## Training overview {.smaller}

| Module | Topic | Exercise |
|--------|-------|----------|
| 01 | Why test | `01_bash/` |
| 02 | Concepts | — |
| 03 | Bash · Python · R | `01_bash/` `02_python/` `03_r/` |
| 04 | nf-test | `04_nextflow/` |
| 06 | Bioinformatics testing | `06_metamorphic/` |
| 07 | Team strategy | — |
| Bonus | Containers | `05_testing_containers/` |

---

## Exercise → Module 01

Go to `exercises/01_bash/`

At the end, you must have: 1 happy path test, 1 empty file test, 1 missing file test, 1 assertion on the output message content, and the bats command documented.
